posts.author: Spunas1
posts.title: Trump interview on passing.

twitter.content: TRUMP: “What makes it even stranger is that I got a call last night sometime … and [Lindsey Graham] said we're all set for the Save America Act … We thought maybe we might even meet today. And then that was it.”

posts.author: Landslide_2020
posts.title: @POTUS on Senator Lindsey Graham.  Where he was becoming really strong was the SAVE Act, and I think he was going to be there very strongly on the Filibuster...he was a strong advocate for SAVE America, and that's what he called me about last night.

twitter.content: .@POTUS on Sen. Lindsey Graham: "Where he was really becoming strong was the SAVE America Act, and I think he was going to be there very strongly on the filibuster... he was a strong advocate for SAVE America, and that's what he called me about last night."

posts.raw_content:
Someone like Senator Graham was actually a very good ally of MAGA. He campaigned hard for Trump in 2020 and was one of his earliest 2024 endorsements, at a time when many others (including countless faggots here), were supporting DeSantis 2024.

He voted with Trump 95% of the time. The only time he didn’t vote with Trump was during the Syria withdrawal. He was arguably Trump’s single best ally in the senate.

Yet here we are on “The Donald” listening to a bunch of retards celebrating his death like they won the Super Bowl. One reason is that many people here are not actually MAGA, they’re Muslim cucks and Israel-obsessed spergs.

But another reason is that meme culture has broken the brains of the dumbest portion of MAGA. They saw old memes we made about Graham being a “cocksucking faggot” and “war hawk” — which were intended to be nothing more than humorous hyperbole — and they took them literally. 

I see this happen all the time. Gen X and millennials make “edgy” memes that are not meant to be taken literally, then some Johnny Come Lately Zoomer comes along and forms serious political opinions based on the memes. 

They have no political education. They’re kids who came from broken Obama-schools. Their literal only knowledge of politics is a bunch of joke memes that their older brother showed them. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Be less retarded.

posts.raw_content:
It occurs to me that whilst contemplating my own red line, and what actions I might, or might not take, when that moment comes, I realised I wasn't exactly sure where my red line is.

I'm English, and whilst I understand this is primarlly is US'ian site, I believe I've earned a smidgeon of respect from my American cousins, in spirit at least, so kindly indulge my slightly whisky sodden contemplation.

I have a very clear idea on the kinds of acts that can be performed in the pursuit of freedom against the tyranny of oppression, and yes, I feel the imminence of the jack boot of totalianarism upon my neck, but I am still not at that stage of desperation.

How then, knowing what I know and what comes next, can I still be waiting you ask? And you are right to ask, because it is the core of the issue I face.

You see, I still have a lot to lose. A devoted, if irascible, wife, a home and a comfortable life in a twee little fucking thatched cottagein the beautiful English countriside, as yet unmarred by the parasites of Islamic pursuasion.

Do I define my line based on the injustices meted out upon others? It certainly feels like I should. But what if those most imposed upon still do not feel the doom that is being visited upon them? Do I act alone and take upon myself the burden of judgement for my entire country?

A part of me feels like I should act and possibly be perceived as an inspiration for insurrection, bit there is another part of me thinking:- 'those other fuckers care nothing for me, so why should I?'.

This isn't really about me, it's about how we justify our actions before God and just how the fuck are we really supposed to know when to act?

There are others who will act in a more political fashion, and I am content to let that play out, but make no mistake, I possess a will to fuck things up to a very significant extent and am reluctant to act until there are no better options.

Since my American cousins have faced this moment before and acted (albeit not in living memory) I am curious as to how modern men will judge the moment ripe to act, now that we are all facing possible extinction within our lifetime?
posts.author: JonathanE
posts.title: Where is your *red* line?
posts.content:
It occurs to me that whilst contemplating my own red line, and what actions I might, or might not take, when that moment comes, I realised I wasn't exactly sure where my red line is.

I'm English, and whilst I understand this is primarlly is US'ian site, I believe I've earned a smidgeon of respect from my American cousins, in spirit at least, so kindly indulge my slightly whisky sodden contemplation.

I have a very clear idea on the kinds of acts that can be performed in the pursuit of freedom against the tyranny of oppression, and yes, I feel the imminence of the jack boot of totalianarism upon my neck, but I am still not at that stage of desperation.

How then, knowing what I know and what comes next, can I still be waiting you ask? And you are right to ask, because it is the core of the issue I face.

You see, I still have a lot to lose. A devoted, if irascible, wife, a home and a comfortable life in a twee little fucking thatched cottagein the beautiful English countriside, as yet unmarred by the parasites of Islamic pursuasion.

Do I define my line based on the injustices meted out upon others? It certainly feels like I should. But what if those most imposed upon still do not feel the doom that is being visited upon them? Do I act alone and take upon myself the burden of judgement for my entire country?

A part of me feels like I should act and possibly be perceived as an inspiration for insurrection, bit there is another part of me thinking:- 'those other fuckers care nothing for me, so why should I?'.

This isn't really about me, it's about how we justify our actions before God and just how the fuck are we really supposed to know when to act?

There are others who will act in a more political fashion, and I am content to let that play out, but make no mistake, I possess a will to fuck things up to a very significant extent and am reluctant to act until there are no better options.

Since my American cousins have faced this moment before and acted (albeit not in living memory) I am curious as to how modern men will judge the moment ripe to act, now that we are all facing possible extinction within our lifetime?
